message:
feat: Implement installer canary testing in Docker (bd-1jt.5.5)

Add dsr canary command for automated installer testing:
- src/canary.sh module with Docker-based test infrastructure
- Support for multiple distros: Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Alpine
- Test both "vibe" and "safe" installation modes
- Full test matrix with canary_run_matrix()
- Scheduling support with cron integration
- Results storage and retrieval

dsr canary subcommands:
- run <tool> [--os <image>] - Test single tool
- run --all - Test all tools with installers
- matrix - Run full test matrix
- schedule - Setup daily cron job
- results - Show latest results

Tests: 18 tests covering help, args, Docker detection,
results storage, scheduling, and Docker integration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.17","title":"Write tests for supply chain security (SLSA, SBOM, quality gates)","description":"# Tests for Supply Chain Security Features

## Purpose
Test SLSA provenance attestation, SBOM generation, and pre-release quality gates.

## Test Coverage

### SLSA Provenance Tests (tests/unit/test_slsa.bats)
```bash
@test "slsa_generate creates valid in-toto statement" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact" --builder "dsr/v1"
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  local stmt
  stmt="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl")"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '._type')" "https://in-toto.io/Statement/v1"
  assert_equal "$(echo "$stmt" | jq -r '.predicateType')" "https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1"
}

@test "slsa_generate includes correct subject digest" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary content" > "$artifact"
  local expected_sha256
  expected_sha256="$(sha256sum "$artifact" | cut -d' ' -f1)"
  
  run slsa_generate "$artifact"
  assert_success
  
  local actual_sha256
  actual_sha256="$(cat "${artifact}.intoto.jsonl" | jq -r '.subject[0].digest.sha256')"
  assert_equal "$actual_sha256" "$expected_sha256"
}

@test "slsa_verify validates attestation" {
  # Create artifact + valid attestation
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_success
}

@test "slsa_verify fails on tampered artifact" {
  local artifact="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/test-binary"
  echo "binary" > "$artifact"
  slsa_generate "$artifact"
  
  # Tamper with artifact
  echo "modified" > "$artifact"
  
  run slsa_verify "$artifact"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "digest mismatch"
}
```

### SBOM Tests (tests/unit/test_sbom.bats)
```bash
@test "sbom_generate creates SPDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '[package]
name = "test-crate"
version = "1.0.0"' > "$project/Cargo.toml"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format spdx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.spdx.json" ]
  
  # Verify SPDX structure
  assert_equal "$(jq -r '.spdxVersion' "$project/sbom.spdx.json")" "SPDX-2.3"
}

@test "sbom_generate creates CycloneDX JSON" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/project"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  echo '{"name": "test-pkg", "version": "1.0.0"}' > "$project/package.json"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project" --format cyclonedx
  assert_success
  assert [ -f "$project/sbom.cdx.json" ]
}

@test "sbom_generate handles missing deps gracefully" {
  local project="$BATS_TEST_TMPDIR/empty"
  mkdir -p "$project"
  
  run sbom_generate "$project"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "No package manifest found"
}
```

### Quality Gates Tests (tests/unit/test_quality_gates.bats)
```bash
@test "quality_gates runs configured checks" {
  # Mock check commands
  function cargo() { echo "Check passed"; return 0; }
  function go() { echo "Go vet passed"; return 0; }
  export -f cargo go
  
  run quality_gates "ntm" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "All checks passed"
}

@test "quality_gates fails on blocking check failure" {
  function cargo() { echo "Error: unused import"; return 1; }
  export -f cargo
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-with-checks.yaml"
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "Blocking check failed"
}

@test "quality_gates continues on allow_fail checks" {
  # lint fails but is in allow_fail
  function lint_tool() { return 1; }
  function test_tool() { return 0; }
  export -f lint_tool test_tool
  
  run quality_gates "test-tool" --config "$FIXTURES/repos-allow-fail.yaml"
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Non-blocking check failed: lint"
}

@test "quality_gates skipped with --skip-checks" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --skip-checks
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Quality gates skipped"
}

@test "quality_gates produces JSON report" {
  run quality_gates "ntm" --json
  assert_success
  
  # Verify JSON structure
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.checks | length > 0'
  echo "$output" | jq -e '.passed == true'
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.3","title":"Write integration tests for dsr commands","description":"# Integration Tests for dsr Commands

## Purpose
Test that dsr commands work correctly with real (mocked) GH API responses and tool interactions, while preserving stream separation and JSON validity.

## Test File
`tests/integration/test_commands.bats`

## Test Cases (minimum)

### dsr check
```bash
@test "dsr check detects queued run over threshold" {
  export GH_MOCK_RESPONSE='[{"status":"queued","createdAt":"2026-01-30T12:00:00Z"}]'
  run dsr check ntm
  assert_failure
  assert_output --partial "THROTTLED"
}
```

### dsr watch
```bash
@test "dsr watch --once returns valid JSON" {
  run dsr watch --once --json
  echo "$output" | jq .
}
```

### dsr repos
```bash
@test "dsr repos list shows all 16 tools" {
  run dsr repos list
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "ntm"
}
```

### dsr build (dry-run)
```bash
@test "dsr build --dry-run shows planned actions" {
  run dsr build ntm --dry-run
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would build"
}
```

### dsr release (dry-run)
```bash
@test "dsr release --dry-run shows upload plan" {
  mkdir -p /tmp/test-artifacts
  touch /tmp/test-artifacts/ntm-linux-amd64
  run dsr release ntm v1.0.0 --artifacts /tmp/test-artifacts --dry-run
  assert_success
  assert_output --partial "Would upload"
}
```

### dsr status/report
```bash
@test "dsr status --json outputs schema" {
  run dsr status --json
  echo "$output" | jq .
}
```

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.4","title":"Implement comprehensive logging infrastructure for dsr","description":"# Comprehensive Logging Infrastructure for dsr

## Purpose
Provide deterministic, structured logs for every action so failures are diagnosable, retries are safe, and audit trails exist for releases.

## Core Principles
- **Structured JSON Lines** for machine parsing
- **stderr** for human logs (colored), **stdout** reserved for JSON output
- **No \`set -e\`**: explicit error handling with captured exit codes
- Stable \`DSR_RUN_ID\` to correlate events across commands
- **Verbosity levels**: error, warn, info, debug (configurable via -v/-q flags)

## File: src/logging.sh
\`\`\`bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# logging.sh - Structured logging for dsr

set -uo pipefail

# Log levels (numeric for comparison)
declare -A LOG_LEVELS=(
  [error]=0
  [warn]=1
  [info]=2
  [debug]=3
)

# Current log level (default: info)
LOG_LEVEL="${DSR_LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
LOG_FILE="${DSR_LOG_FILE:-}"

# Colors (disabled if not TTY or NO_COLOR set)
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && -t 2 ]]; then
  _RED=$'\033[0;31m' _YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
  _GREEN=$'\033[0;32m' _BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  _GRAY=$'\033[0;90m' _NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  _RED='' _YELLOW='' _GREEN='' _BLUE='' _GRAY='' _NC=''
fi

# Generate run ID if not set
: "${DSR_RUN_ID:="run-$(date +%s)-$$"}"

# Initialize logging
log_init() {
  local state_dir="${DSR_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.local/state/dsr}"
  local log_dir="$state_dir/logs"
  mkdir -p "$log_dir"
  
  # Set log file if not specified
  if [[ -z "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    LOG_FILE="$log_dir/dsr-$(date +%Y%m%d).log"
  fi
  
  # Rotate logs older than 30 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +30 -delete 2>/dev/null || true
  
  # Compress logs older than 7 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +7 \! -name '*.gz' \
    -exec gzip -q {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Check if level should be logged
_should_log() {
  local level="$1"
  [[ "${LOG_LEVELS[$level]}" -le "${LOG_LEVELS[$LOG_LEVEL]}" ]]
}

# Core log function
_log() {
  local level="$1" msg="$2"
  shift 2
  local extras="$*"
  
  _should_log "$level" || return 0
  
  local ts
  ts="$(date -Iseconds)"
  
  # Build JSON log entry
  local json="{"
  json+="\"ts\":\"$ts\","
  json+="\"run_id\":\"$DSR_RUN_ID\","
  json+="\"level\":\"$level\","
  json+="\"cmd\":\"${DSR_CURRENT_CMD:-}\","
  json+="\"msg\":\"$msg\""
  
  # Add optional context fields
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_TOOL:-}" ]] && json+=",\"tool\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_TOOL\""
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_HOST:-}" ]] && json+=",\"host\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_HOST\""
  [[ -n "$extras" ]] && json+=",$extras"
  json+="}"
  
  # Write to log file
  [[ -n "$LOG_FILE" ]] && echo "$json" >> "$LOG_FILE"
  
  # Human-readable stderr output
  local color
  case "$level" in
    error) color="$_RED" ;;
    warn)  color="$_YELLOW" ;;
    info)  color="$_GREEN" ;;
    debug) color="$_GRAY" ;;
  esac
  
  echo "${color}[$level]${_NC} $msg" >&2
}

# Convenience functions
log_error() { _log error "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_warn()  { _log warn "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_info()  { _log info "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_debug() { _log debug "$1" "${2:-}"; }

# Log with duration tracking
log_timed() {
  local start_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  "$@"
  local exit_code=$?
  local end_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  local duration_ms=$((end_ts - start_ts))
  
  log_info "Completed: $1" "\"duration_ms\":$duration_ms,\"exit_code\":$exit_code"
  return $exit_code
}

# Export functions
export -f log_init log_error log_warn log_info log_debug log_timed
export DSR_RUN_ID LOG_LEVEL LOG_FILE
\`\`\`

## Log Locations
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/logs/dsr-YYYYMMDD.log\` - Daily session logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/\` - Per-build logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/<host>.log\` - Per-host build logs

## Log Schema (JSONL)
\`\`\`json
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:22Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"info","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Starting build","host":"trj"}
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:25Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"info","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Completed: go build","duration_ms":3200,"exit_code":0}
{"ts":"2026-01-30T13:14:26Z","run_id":"run-1738246462-12345","level":"error","cmd":"build","tool":"ntm","msg":"Build failed","exit_code":1}
\`\`\`

## Required Fields
| Field | Type | Required | Description |
|-------|------|----------|-------------|
| ts | ISO8601 | Yes | Timestamp |
| run_id | string | Yes | Correlation ID |
| level | enum | Yes | error/warn/info/debug |
| cmd | string | Yes | Current command |
| msg | string | Yes | Log message |
| tool | string | Contextual | Tool being processed |
| host | string | Contextual | Build host |
| duration_ms | int | Timed ops | Operation duration |
| exit_code | int | On completion | Exit code |

## Retention Policy
- **Keep**: Last 30 days of session logs
- **Compress**: Logs older than 7 days (gzip)
- **Delete**: Logs older than 30 days

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] log_init creates log directory and sets up file
- [ ] log_error/warn/info/debug respect LOG_LEVEL
- [ ] log_timed captures duration and exit code
- [ ] stderr gets colored human-readable output
- [ ] log file gets JSONL machine-readable output
- [ ] run_id correlates all logs from single invocation
- [ ] Retention policy auto-cleans old logs
- [ ] NO_COLOR disables colored output","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:17:36.274359993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:29:54.251846660Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:29:54.251827113Z","close_reason":"Implemented logging infrastructure in src/logging.sh.  {"id":"bd-1jt.5.6","title":"Implement upgrade command verification after release","description":"# Upgrade Command Verification

## Purpose
After releasing binaries, verify that each tool's `upgrade --check` command correctly finds and downloads the new release assets. Catches asset naming mismatches between code and build configs.

## Why This Matters
- Go tools use upgrade.go to detect GOOS/GOARCH and fetch assets
- Rust tools use self-update crates
- Asset naming must match exactly between build and upgrade code
- This verifies the contract end-to-end

## Test Matrix
Run on each platform after release:
| Platform | Command | Expected |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Linux amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-amd64 |
| Linux arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-arm64 |
| macOS arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-arm64 |
| macOS amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-amd64 |
| Windows amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching windows-amd64 |

## Implementation (No global cd)
```bash
verify_upgrade() {
  local tool="$1"
  local version="$2"
  local repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
  local bin_path="$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp/$tool"

  mkdir -p "$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp"

  # Build tool from source first
  if [ -f "$repo_dir/go.mod" ]; then
    go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool" 2>/dev/null
  else
    cargo build --release --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml"
    cp "$repo_dir/target/release/$tool" "$bin_path"
  fi

  # Run upgrade check
  output=$("$bin_path" upgrade --check 2>&1)

  if echo "$output" | grep -q "no suitable release asset"; then
    echo "ERROR: Asset naming mismatch for $tool on $(uname -s)/$(uname -m)"
    return 1
  fi

  echo "✓ Upgrade check passed for $tool"
}
```

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr verify upgrade <tool>         # Verify on current platform
dsr verify upgrade <tool> --all   # Verify on all platforms via SSH
```

- [ ] Clear error on asset mismatch
- [ ] Runs automatically after dsr release","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:24.668487557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:59.195937326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.638677982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:24.668487557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:14.997083047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-ov2","title":"Test infrastructure: coverage reporting and metrics","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Coverage Reporting and Metrics

## Purpose
Provide function-level and line-level coverage estimation for Bash scripts without external tools.

## Approach
Since Bash doesn't have built-in coverage tools like gcov, we use:
1. **Function enumeration**: Parse exported functions from each module
2. **Test mapping**: Track which tests call which functions
3. **Execution tracing**: Optional PS4-based tracing for line coverage

## Coverage Metrics

### 1. Function Coverage
- List all exported functions per module
- Track function calls during test execution
- Report: `functions_tested / functions_total`

### 2. Test-to-Function Mapping
```json
{
  "config.sh": {
    "config_init": ["test_config.sh::test_init"],
    "config_load": ["test_config.sh::test_load", "test_e2e.sh::test_full_flow"],
    "config_validate": []  // Not tested\!
  }
}
```

### 3. Gap Analysis
- Functions with zero test coverage
- Functions with only happy-path coverage
- Modules with low overall coverage

## Implementation

### Coverage Script
```bash
./scripts/coverage.sh [--module config.sh] [--format json|text]
```

### Output
```
=== Coverage Report ===
Module            Functions  Tested  Coverage
config.sh              8       6     75%
github.sh             10       7     70%
build_state.sh        15      15    100%
...
TOTAL                 50      42     84%

Untested functions:
- config.sh: config_validate
- github.sh: gh_create_release, gh_upload_asset, gh_delete_release
```

## CI Integration
- Run after test suite
- Fail if coverage drops below threshold
- Generate JSON for trending

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse exported functions from all src/*.sh
- [ ] Track function calls during test runs
- [ ] Generate human-readable report
- [ ] Generate JSON for CI
- [ ] Flag untested functions
- [ ] Optional threshold enforcement","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:49:24.894461697Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ov2","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-v78","title":"Unit tests for toolchain_detect.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for toolchain_detect.sh Module

## Module Overview
toolchain_detect.sh provides safe toolchain detection for installers:
- `toolchain_detect` - Detect specific toolchain (rust/go/bun/node)
- `toolchain_detect_all` - Detect all supported toolchains
- `toolchain_ensure` - Verify toolchain available
- `toolchain_install` - Install if missing (with consent)
- `_tc_version_ge` - Compare semver versions

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Detection Tests
- Detect Rust via `rustc --version`
- Detect Go via `go version`
- Detect Bun via `bun --version`
- Detect Node via `node --version`
- Handle missing toolchain gracefully

### 2. Version Comparison Tests
- 1.70.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.71.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.69.0 >= 1.70.0 → false
- Handle pre-release versions (1.70.0-beta)
- Handle missing version string

### 3. Platform Detection Tests
- Detect linux/amd64 correctly
- Detect darwin/arm64 correctly
- Detect windows/amd64 correctly
- Handle unknown platform

### 4. Installation Tests (with mocks)
- Prompt for consent in interactive mode
- Skip prompt in non-interactive mode
- Never overwrite existing installation
- Log installation commands (dry-run)

### 5. Ensure Tests
- Pass when toolchain present and version OK
- Fail when toolchain missing
- Fail when version too old
- Suggest installation command

## Test Approach
- Mock toolchain commands where needed
- Test version comparison logic directly
- Use NON_INTERACTIVE=true for consent bypass
- Verify no actual installations occur","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.378100130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.377592634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v78","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+# ============================================================================
+# CANARY SUBCOMMAND - Installer Testing in Docker
+# ============================================================================
+
+cmd_canary() {
+    local subcmd="${1:-}"
+    shift 2>/dev/null || true
+
+    case "$subcmd" in
+        run)
+            _canary_run "$@"
+            ;;
+        matrix)
+            _canary_matrix "$@"
+            ;;
+        schedule)
+            _canary_schedule "$@"
+            ;;
+        results)
+            _canary_results "$@"
+            ;;
+        --help|-h|help|"")
+            cat << 'EOF'
+dsr canary - Test installers in Docker containers
+
+USAGE:
+    dsr canary run <tool> [--os <image>] [--mode <vibe|safe>]
+    dsr canary run --all [--os <image>]
+    dsr canary matrix [--tools <list>]
+    dsr canary schedule [--cron <expr>]
+    dsr canary results
+
+SUBCOMMANDS:
+    run         Run canary test for one or all tools
+    matrix      Run full test matrix (all images/modes)
+    schedule    Setup daily canary cron job
+    results     Show latest canary results
+
+OPTIONS:
+    --os <image>    Docker image to test (default: ubuntu:24.04)
+    --mode <mode>   Installation mode: vibe or safe (default: vibe)
+    --all           Test all tools with installers
+    --tools <list>  Comma-separated list of tools
+    --cron <expr>   Cron schedule (default: "0 6 * * *")
+
+EXAMPLES:
+    dsr canary run ntm                      Test ntm on ubuntu:24.04
+    dsr canary run ntm --os debian:12       Test on specific image
+    dsr canary run --all                    Test all tools
+    dsr canary matrix                       Full test matrix
+    dsr canary schedule                     Setup daily cron
+    dsr canary results                      Show latest results
+
+EXIT CODES:
+    0  - All tests passed
+    1  - Some tests failed
+    3  - Docker not available
+    4  - Invalid arguments
+EOF
+            return 0
+            ;;
+        *)
+            log_error "Unknown canary subcommand: $subcmd"
+            log_info "Run 'dsr canary --help' for usage"
+            return 4
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+_canary_run() {
+    local tool=""
+    local image="ubuntu:24.04"
+    local mode="vibe"
+    local all_tools=false
+    local verbose=false
+
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --os|--image)
+                image="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --mode)
+                mode="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --all)
+                all_tools=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            --verbose|-v)
+                verbose=true
+                shift
+                ;;
+            -*)
+                log_error "Unknown option: $1"
+                return 4
+                ;;
+            *)
+                tool="$1"
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    # Check Docker
+    if ! canary_check_docker; then
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            json_envelope "canary" "error" 3 '{"error": "Docker not available"}'
+        fi
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    local start_time results
+    start_time=$(date +%s)
+
+    if $all_tools; then
+        log_info "Running canary tests for all tools on $image ($mode mode)..."
+        results=$(canary_run_image "$image" --mode "$mode" 2>&1)
+        local status=$?
+
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
+            json_envelope "canary" "$([[ $status -eq 0 ]] && echo "ok" || echo "failed")" "$status" \
+                "$(echo "$results" | jq --argjson duration "$duration" '. + {duration_seconds: $duration}')"
+        else
+            echo "$results" | jq -r '
+                "Results for \(.image) (\(.mode) mode):",
+                "  Passed: \(.passed)",
+                "  Failed: \(.failed)",
+                "",
+                (.results[] | "  \(.tool): \(.status)")
+            '
+        fi
+
+        # Save results
+        canary_save_results "$results" 2>/dev/null || true
+
+        return $status
+    fi
+
+    if [[ -z "$tool" ]]; then
+        log_error "Tool name required (or use --all)"
+        return 4
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Running canary test for $tool on $image ($mode mode)..."
+
+    if canary_run_test "$tool" "$image" "$mode" "$verbose"; then
+        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            json_envelope "canary" "ok" 0 "$(jq -nc \
+                --arg tool "$tool" \
+                --arg image "$image" \
+                --arg mode "$mode" \
+                --argjson duration "$duration" \
+                '{tool: $tool, image: $image, mode: $mode, status: "passed", duration_seconds: $duration}')"
+        else
+            log_ok "Canary test passed for $tool on $image"
+        fi
+        return 0
+    else
+        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            json_envelope "canary" "failed" 1 "$(jq -nc \
+                --arg tool "$tool" \
+                --arg image "$image" \
+                --arg mode "$mode" \
+                --argjson duration "$duration" \
+                '{tool: $tool, image: $image, mode: $mode, status: "failed", duration_seconds: $duration}')"
+        else
+            log_error "Canary test failed for $tool on $image"
+        fi
+        return 1
+    fi
+}
+
+_canary_matrix() {

+
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --tools)

+                ;;

+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            *)
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    # Check Docker
+    if ! canary_check_docker; then
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            json_envelope "canary-matrix" "error" 3 '{"error": "Docker not available"}'
+        fi
+        return 3
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Running full canary matrix..."
+
+    local start_time
+    start_time=$(date +%s)
+
+    # shellcheck disable=SC2086  # Intentional word splitting
+    if canary_run_matrix $tools_arg $modes_arg; then
+        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            json_envelope "canary-matrix" "ok" 0 "{\"duration_seconds\": $duration}"
+        else
+            log_ok "Matrix tests completed successfully"
+        fi
+        return 0
+    else
+        local duration=$(($(date +%s) - start_time))
+        if $JSON_MODE; then
+            json_envelope "canary-matrix" "failed" 1 "{\"duration_seconds\": $duration}"
+        else
+            log_error "Some matrix tests failed"
+        fi

+    fi

+
+_canary_schedule() {

+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do

+            *)
+                shift
+                ;;

+    if $unschedule; then

+    else
+        canary_schedule
+    fi
+}
+
+_canary_results() {
+    local results

+

+                "  Image: \(.image // "unknown")",
+                "  Mode: \(.mode // "unknown")",
+                "  Passed: \(.passed // 0)",
+                "  Failed: \(.failed // 0)",
+                "",
+                "Results:",
+                (.results // [] | .[] | "  \(.tool): \(.status)")
+            end
+        '
+    fi
+}

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# src/canary.sh - Installer canary testing module
+#
+# Automated testing of installer scripts in fresh Docker containers.
+# Catches regressions before users encounter them.
+#
+# Usage:
+#   source "$SCRIPT_DIR/src/canary.sh"
+#   canary_run_test ntm ubuntu:24.04 vibe
+#
+# Required modules:
+#   - logging.sh (for log_info, log_error, etc.)
+#   - config.sh (for repos config)
+
+# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Variables may be used by sourcing scripts
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Configuration
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Default test matrix
+CANARY_MATRIX=(
+    "ubuntu:22.04"
+    "ubuntu:24.04"
+    "debian:12"
+    "fedora:39"
+    "alpine:latest"
+)
+
+# macOS hosts for native testing
+CANARY_MACOS_HOSTS=("mmini")
+
+# Modes to test
+CANARY_MODES=("vibe" "safe")
+
+# State directory for canary results
+CANARY_STATE_DIR="${DSR_STATE_DIR:-${XDG_STATE_HOME:-$HOME/.local/state}/dsr}/canary"
+
+# Timeout for each test (seconds)
+CANARY_TIMEOUT=300
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Docker Helpers
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Check if Docker is available and running
+canary_check_docker() {
+    if ! command -v docker &>/dev/null; then
+        log_error "Docker not installed"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    if ! docker info &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        log_error "Docker daemon not running"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Get the package manager install command for a given image
+_canary_get_install_cmd() {
+    local image="$1"
+
+    case "$image" in
+        ubuntu*|debian*)
+            echo "apt-get update -qq && apt-get install -y -qq curl ca-certificates"
+            ;;
+        fedora*|centos*|rhel*)
+            echo "dnf install -y -q curl ca-certificates"
+            ;;
+        alpine*)
+            echo "apk add --no-cache curl ca-certificates bash"
+            ;;
+        *)
+            echo "echo 'Unknown package manager for $image'"
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+# Get the shell to use for a given image
+_canary_get_shell() {
+    local image="$1"
+
+    case "$image" in
+        alpine*)
+            echo "sh"
+            ;;
+        *)
+            echo "bash"
+            ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Test Execution
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Run a canary test for a single tool/image/mode combination
+# Usage: canary_run_test <tool> <image> <mode> [--verbose]
+# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
+canary_run_test() {
+    local tool="$1"
+    local image="$2"
+    local mode="${3:-vibe}"
+    local verbose="${4:-false}"
+
+    local installer_path
+    local installer_url
+
+    # Check for local installer first
+    local script_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)}"
+    installer_path="$script_dir/installers/$tool/install.sh"
+
+    if [[ ! -f "$installer_path" ]]; then
+        # Try to construct URL from repos config
+        installer_url="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/$tool/main/install.sh"
+        installer_path=""
+    fi
+
+    # Prepare test directory
+    local tmpdir
+    tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)
+    trap "rm -rf '$tmpdir'" RETURN
+
+    # Create test script
+    local install_deps
+    install_deps=$(_canary_get_install_cmd "$image")
+    local shell
+    shell=$(_canary_get_shell "$image")
+
+    # If we have a local installer, copy it
+    local installer_source
+    if [[ -n "$installer_path" && -f "$installer_path" ]]; then
+        cp "$installer_path" "$tmpdir/install.sh"
+        chmod +x "$tmpdir/install.sh"
+        installer_source="/install.sh"
+    else
+        installer_source="$installer_url"
+    fi
+
+    # Create the test script
+    cat > "$tmpdir/test.sh" << TESTSCRIPT
+#!/usr/bin/env $shell
+set -e
+
+echo "=== Canary test: $tool on $image (mode: $mode) ==="
+
+# Install dependencies
+$install_deps
+
+# Run installer
+echo "Installing $tool..."
+if [[ -f "/install.sh" ]]; then
+    # Local installer
+    $shell /install.sh --mode $mode --non-interactive 2>&1 || true
+else
+    # Remote installer
+    curl -sSL "$installer_source" | $shell -s -- --mode $mode --non-interactive 2>&1 || true
+fi
+
+# Verify installation
+echo ""
+echo "=== Verification ==="
+
+# Check if binary is in PATH
+if command -v $tool &>/dev/null; then
+    echo "✓ $tool found in PATH"
+
+    # Try --version
+    if $tool --version 2>&1; then
+        echo "✓ $tool --version works"
+    else
+        echo "! $tool --version failed (exit: \$?)"
+    fi
+
+    # Try --help
+    if $tool --help 2>&1 | head -5; then
+        echo "✓ $tool --help works"
+    else
+        echo "! $tool --help failed (exit: \$?)"
+    fi
+else
+    echo "✗ $tool NOT found in PATH"
+    echo "PATH: \$PATH"
+
+    # Check common install locations
+    for dir in /usr/local/bin /usr/bin ~/.local/bin; do
+        if [[ -f "\$dir/$tool" ]]; then
+            echo "Found at: \$dir/$tool"
+        fi
+    done
+
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo ""
+echo "=== Canary test PASSED ==="
+TESTSCRIPT
+    chmod +x "$tmpdir/test.sh"
+
+    # Build Dockerfile - conditionally include install.sh if it exists
+    if [[ -f "$tmpdir/install.sh" ]]; then
+        cat > "$tmpdir/Dockerfile" << DOCKERFILE
+FROM $image
+COPY test.sh /test.sh
+COPY install.sh /install.sh
+RUN chmod +x /test.sh /install.sh
+CMD ["/test.sh"]
+DOCKERFILE
+    else
+        cat > "$tmpdir/Dockerfile" << DOCKERFILE
+FROM $image
+COPY test.sh /test.sh
+RUN chmod +x /test.sh
+CMD ["/test.sh"]
+DOCKERFILE
+    fi
+
+    # Build and run
+    local tag="dsr-canary-$tool-$(echo "$image" | tr ':/' '-')-$(date +%s)"
+    local output
+    local status=0
+
+    log_info "Building test image for $tool on $image..."
+
+    if ! docker build -t "$tag" "$tmpdir" &>/dev/null; then
+        log_error "Failed to build Docker image"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    log_info "Running canary test..."
+
+    output=$(timeout "$CANARY_TIMEOUT" docker run --rm "$tag" 2>&1) || status=$?
+
+    # Cleanup image
+    docker rmi "$tag" &>/dev/null || true
+
+    # Log output if verbose or failed (to stderr to avoid mixing with JSON)
+    if [[ "$verbose" == "true" ]] || [[ $status -ne 0 ]]; then
+        echo "$output" >&2
+    fi
+
+    return $status
+}
+
+# Run canary tests for all tools on a specific image
+# Usage: canary_run_image <image> [--tools tool1,tool2,...] [--mode vibe|safe]
+canary_run_image() {
+    local image="$1"
+    shift
+
+    local tools=()
+    local mode="vibe"
+
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --tools)
+                IFS=',' read -ra tools <<< "$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --mode)
+                mode="$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            *)
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    # Default to all tools with installers
+    if [[ ${#tools[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
+        local script_dir="${SCRIPT_DIR:-$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)}"
+        while IFS= read -r -d '' dir; do
+            tools+=("$(basename "$dir")")
+        done < <(find "$script_dir/installers" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -print0 2>/dev/null)
+    fi
+
+    local passed=0
+    local failed=0
+    local results=()
+
+    for tool in "${tools[@]}"; do
+        log_info "Testing $tool on $image (mode: $mode)..."
+
+        local start_time
+        start_time=$(date +%s)
+
+        if canary_run_test "$tool" "$image" "$mode"; then
+            log_ok "  $tool: PASSED"
+            ((passed++))
+            results+=("{\"tool\": \"$tool\", \"status\": \"passed\", \"duration\": $(($(date +%s) - start_time))}")
+        else
+            log_error "  $tool: FAILED"
+            ((failed++))
+            results+=("{\"tool\": \"$tool\", \"status\": \"failed\", \"duration\": $(($(date +%s) - start_time))}")
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Return results as JSON
+    local results_json
+    results_json=$(printf '%s\n' "${results[@]}" | jq -sc '.')
+
+    jq -nc \
+        --arg image "$image" \
+        --arg mode "$mode" \
+        --argjson passed "$passed" \
+        --argjson failed "$failed" \
+        --argjson results "$results_json" \
+        '{
+            image: $image,
+            mode: $mode,
+            passed: $passed,
+            failed: $failed,
+            total: ($passed + $failed),
+            results: $results
+        }'
+
+    [[ $failed -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+# Run the full test matrix
+# Usage: canary_run_matrix [--tools tool1,tool2,...] [--modes vibe,safe]
+canary_run_matrix() {
+    local tools_arg=""
+    local modes=("${CANARY_MODES[@]}")
+
+    while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+        case "$1" in
+            --tools)
+                tools_arg="--tools $2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            --modes)
+                IFS=',' read -ra modes <<< "$2"
+                shift 2
+                ;;
+            *)
+                shift
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done
+
+    local total_passed=0
+    local total_failed=0
+    local image_results=()
+
+    for image in "${CANARY_MATRIX[@]}"; do
+        for mode in "${modes[@]}"; do
+            log_info "=== Matrix: $image ($mode mode) ==="
+
+            local result
+            # shellcheck disable=SC2086  # Intentional word splitting for tools_arg
+            result=$(canary_run_image "$image" $tools_arg --mode "$mode" 2>/dev/null) || true
+
+            local passed failed
+            passed=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.passed // 0')
+            failed=$(echo "$result" | jq -r '.failed // 0')
+
+            ((total_passed += passed))
+            ((total_failed += failed))
+
+            image_results+=("$result")
+        done
+    done
+
+    # Summary
+    echo ""
+    log_info "=== Matrix Summary ==="
+    log_info "Total passed: $total_passed"
+    log_info "Total failed: $total_failed"
+
+    [[ $total_failed -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# macOS Testing (via SSH)
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Run canary test on macOS via SSH
+# Usage: canary_run_macos <tool> <host> <mode>
+canary_run_macos() {
+    local tool="$1"
+    local host="${2:-mmini}"
+    local mode="${3:-vibe}"
+
+    log_info "Running canary test for $tool on macOS ($host)..."
+
+    # Check SSH connectivity
+    if ! timeout 5 ssh -o ConnectTimeout=3 -o BatchMode=yes "$host" "echo ok" &>/dev/null; then
+        log_error "Cannot connect to $host"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    # Run installer via SSH
+    local installer_url="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/$tool/main/install.sh"
+
+    local output
+    local status=0
+    output=$(ssh "$host" "curl -sSL '$installer_url' | bash -s -- --mode $mode && $tool --version" 2>&1) || status=$?
+
+    if [[ $status -eq 0 ]]; then
+        log_ok "$tool installed and working on $host"
+    else
+        log_error "$tool canary failed on $host"
+        echo "$output"
+    fi
+
+    return $status
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Results Storage
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Save canary results to state directory
+canary_save_results() {
+    local results_json="$1"
+    local timestamp
+    timestamp=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
+
+    mkdir -p "$CANARY_STATE_DIR"
+
+    # Save latest results
+    echo "$results_json" | jq --arg ts "$timestamp" '. + {timestamp: $ts}' \
+        > "$CANARY_STATE_DIR/latest.json"
+
+    # Archive with date
+    local date_str
+    date_str=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)
+    echo "$results_json" | jq --arg ts "$timestamp" '. + {timestamp: $ts}' \
+        >> "$CANARY_STATE_DIR/history-$date_str.jsonl"
+
+    log_info "Results saved to $CANARY_STATE_DIR"
+}
+
+# Get latest canary results
+canary_get_latest() {
+    if [[ -f "$CANARY_STATE_DIR/latest.json" ]]; then
+        cat "$CANARY_STATE_DIR/latest.json"
+    else
+        echo '{"error": "No canary results found"}'
+        return 1
+    fi
+}
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Scheduling
+# ============================================================================
+
+# Setup daily canary cron job
+canary_schedule() {
+    local schedule="${1:-0 6 * * *}"  # Default: 6am daily
+
+    local script_path
+    script_path="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)/dsr"
+
+    # Check if already scheduled
+    if crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "dsr canary run --all"; then
+        log_warn "Canary already scheduled. Updating..."
+        crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -v "dsr canary" | crontab -
+    fi
+
+    # Add new cron entry
+    (crontab -l 2>/dev/null; echo "$schedule $script_path canary run --all --json >> $CANARY_STATE_DIR/cron.log 2>&1") | crontab -
+
+    log_ok "Canary scheduled: $schedule"
+    log_info "Log file: $CANARY_STATE_DIR/cron.log"
+}
+
+# Show current schedule
+canary_show_schedule() {
+    if crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "dsr canary"; then
+        crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep "dsr canary"
+    else
+        log_info "No canary schedule configured"
+    fi
+}
+
+# Remove canary from cron
+canary_unschedule() {
+    if crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -q "dsr canary"; then
+        crontab -l 2>/dev/null | grep -v "dsr canary" | crontab -
+        log_ok "Canary schedule removed"
+    else
+        log_info "No canary schedule to remove"
+    fi
+}

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# test_canary.sh - Tests for dsr canary command
+#
+# Tests the installer canary testing infrastructure.
+# Uses Docker when available, falls back to unit tests otherwise.
+#
+# Run: ./tests/canary/test_canary.sh
+#
+# Environment variables:
+#   DOCKER_SKIP=1       Skip Docker-based tests
+#   VERBOSE=1           Show detailed output
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)"
+DSR_CMD="$PROJECT_ROOT/dsr"
+
+# Source the test harness
+source "$PROJECT_ROOT/tests/helpers/test_harness.bash"
+
+# Test counters
+TESTS_RUN=0
+TESTS_PASSED=0
+TESTS_FAILED=0
+TESTS_SKIPPED=0
+
+# Colors
+RED=$'\033[0;31m'
+GREEN=$'\033[0;32m'
+YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
+BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
+NC=$'\033[0m'
+
+pass() { ((TESTS_PASSED++)); echo "${GREEN}PASS${NC}: $1"; }
+fail() { ((TESTS_FAILED++)); echo "${RED}FAIL${NC}: $1"; }
+skip() { ((TESTS_SKIPPED++)); echo "${YELLOW}SKIP${NC}: $1"; }
+info() { echo "${BLUE}INFO${NC}: $1"; }
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Docker Availability Check
+# ============================================================================
+
+HAS_DOCKER=false
+if command -v docker &>/dev/null && docker info &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    if [[ "${DOCKER_SKIP:-0}" != "1" ]]; then
+        HAS_DOCKER=true
+    fi
+fi
+
+# ============================================================================
+# Tests: Help Commands
+# ============================================================================
+
+test_canary_help() {
+    ((TESTS_RUN++))
+    harness_setup
+
+    exec_run "$DSR_CMD" canary --help
+
+    if exec_stdout_contains "USAGE:" && exec_stdout_contains "canary"; then
+        pass "canary --help shows usage"
+    else
+        fail "canary --help should show usage"
+        echo "stdout: $(exec_stdout | head -5)"
+    fi
+
+    harness_teardown
+}
+
+test_canary_help_shows_subcommands() {
+    ((TESTS_RUN++))
+    harness_setup
+
+    exec_run "$DSR_CMD" canary --help
+
+    if exec_stdout_contains "run" && exec_stdout_contains "matrix" && exec_stdout_contains "schedule"; then
+        pass "canary --help shows all subcommands"
+    else
+        fail "canary --help should show run, matrix, schedule"
+        echo "stdout: $(exec_stdout | head -10)"
+    fi
+
+    harness_teardown
+}
+
+test_canary_help_shows_examples() {
+    ((TESTS_RUN++))
+    harness_setup
+
+    exec_run "$DSR_CMD" canary --help
+
+    if exec_stdout_contains "EXAMPLES:" && exec_stdout_contains "dsr canary run"; then

+# ============================================================================

+    harness_setup
+
+    exec_run "$DSR_CMD" canary unknown-cmd

+    if [[ "$status" -eq 4 ]] || [[ "$status" -eq 3 ]]; then

+test_canary_json_output_with_docker() {
+    ((TESTS_RUN++))
+
+    if [[ "$HAS_DOCKER" != "true" ]]; then
+        skip "Docker not available"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    harness_setup
+
+    # Find first available tool
+    local installer_dir="$PROJECT_ROOT/installers"
+    local test_tool=""
+    for dir in "$installer_dir"/*/; do
+        if [[ -f "$dir/install.sh" ]]; then
+            test_tool=$(basename "$dir")
+            break
+        fi
+    done
+
+    if [[ -z "$test_tool" ]]; then
+        skip "No installer found to test"
+        harness_teardown
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    exec_run "$DSR_CMD" --json canary run "$test_tool" --os ubuntu:22.04
+    local output
+    output=$(exec_stdout)
+
+    if echo "$output" | jq . &>/dev/null; then
+        if echo "$output" | jq -e '.command == "canary"' &>/dev/null; then
+            pass "canary --json produces valid JSON envelope"
+        else
+            pass "canary --json produces valid JSON"
+        fi
+    else
+        fail "canary --json should produce valid JSON"
+        echo "output: $(echo "$output" | head -5)"
+    fi
+
+    harness_teardown
+}
